content.full_text:
McDONOUGH, HOLLAND & ALLEN
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
ATTORNEYS

555 CAPITOL MALL, SUITE 950
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 444-3900
TELECOPIER (916) 444-8334

YUBA CITY OFFICE
1885 BUTTE HOUSE ROAD
YUBA CITY, CALIFORNIA 95991
(916) 674-0796
TELECOPIER (916) 674-0990

BAY AREA OFFICE
1999 HARRISON STREET, SUITE 1300
OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94612
(415) 832-0500
TELECOPIER (415) 839-9104

August 15, 1990

Kathryn Donovan
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice (Gov. Code § 83114(b))

Dear Ms. Donovan:

On behalf of Sierra County Supervisor Craig McHenry, I am writing to request advice pursuant to Government Code section 83114(b).

The facts are as follows. Supervisor McHenry has established a family trust, the sole beneficiaries of which are his three minor children. The family trust owns all of the stock of a corporation which owns certain real property within Sierra County. The corporation has applied for a conditional use permit to allow for the storage and repair of logging equipment on its property. If the conditional use permit is granted, the corporation would lease the property to Edward Hood, who would store and repair his company's trucks on the property. Mr. Hood has leased the property from the corporation in the past when the applicant was originally granted a similar use permit.

Supervisor McHenry is an attorney. He has represented and continues to represent Mr. Hood, and therefore, Mr. Hood is a source of income to him. In addition to his logging business, Mr Hood has a garbage hauling business, and has a hauling contract with Sierra County. Supervisor McHenry has never voted on the hauling contract or its renewal when it was before the Board of Supervisors. Supervisor McHenry also understands that he may not

Kathryn Donovan
August 15, 1990
Page 2


vote as a member of the Board of Supervisors on the conditional use permit
application.

The question is whether Supervisor McHenry may represent the
applicant for the use permit in his personal capacity before both the County
Planning Commission and the Board of Supervisors pursuant to 2 Cal. Code
Regs. section 18700.1(b). As I mentioned above, the applicant is a corporation
which is wholly owned by the family trust, the sole beneficiaries of which are
Supervisor McHenry's minor children.

Thank you very much for your assistance. Please let me know if you
need any additional information.

Very truly yours,


Iris P. Yang

IPY:lgs

cc:    Supervisor Craig McHenry
       William Pangman, Sierra County Counsel

sections.question: The question is whether Supervisor McHenry may represent the applicant for the use permit in his personal capacity before both the County Planning Commission and the Board of Supervisors pursuant to 2 Cal. Code Regs. section 18700.1(b). As I mentioned above, the applicant is a corporation which is wholly owned by the family trust, the sole beneficiaries of which are Supervisor McHenry's minor children.
sections.question_synthetic: Can Supervisor McHenry represent a corporation (owned by a family trust benefiting his minor children) before the Planning Commission and Board of Supervisors for a conditional use permit, given his financial interest in the applicant through past representation of the applicant lessee and his indirect interest through his children's trust?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section present. Question clearly stated in final paragraph. Document dated August 15, 1990.

embedding.summary: Attorney Iris P. Yang requests FPPC advice on behalf of Sierra County Supervisor Craig McHenry regarding whether he may represent a corporation in a conditional use permit application before the Planning Commission and Board of Supervisors, where the corporation is wholly owned by a family trust for the supervisor's minor children.
